svg.title: Spoovel tier expansion diagram v2 — with advancement rule
svg.desc: Five tiers of Spoovel development. A bold advancement rule between Tier 0 and Tier 1 states: do not move to Tier 1 until Tier 0 beats or meaningfully improves the hand-filled baseline in at least one measurable way. Tier 0 is the minimum viable field tool. Tier 1 adds crank or conveyor assist. Tier 2 adds tie-off and counting and marking. Tier 3 integrates CodeTape and Signal Finder. Tier 4 is AI-assisted planning, an optional future layer only.
svg.text: Spoovel — tier expansion map
Build in order. Measure before advancing. The danger is skipping to Tier 4.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should I build first for Spoovel v0?')
g.text: · Sloped chute (plywood or tarp on frame) · Bag-mouth holder (rebar or bolt forks)
· Wheel base for moving loaded frame · Push handles at body height
· Rope tie-downs for transport · No motor · no battery · no proprietary part
Measure: time per 20 sacks · spills · rework · body strain · repairs needed

svg.text: T0
Advancement rule — must pass before Tier 1
Do not move to Tier 1 until Tier 0 beats or meaningfully improves the hand-filled
baseline in at least one measurable way: time · strain · spills · or access.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does Tier 1 add to the Spoovel?')
g.text: · Hand-crank winch to raise or lower chute angle on the fly
· Short belt or roller conveyor at chute input — bicycle chain or reused belt
· Adjustable chute angle brackets (slotted bolt holes)

svg.text: T1

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does Tier 2 add to the Spoovel?')
g.text: · Clamp or jig for tying off bag tops quickly (one-handed if possible)
· Tally counter or marked board — track bags filled per session
· Paper Tag mount point — attach CodeTape tag to each filled bag or batch

svg.text: T2

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Tier 3 connect Spoovel to CodeTape and Signal Finder?')
g.text: Tier 3 — CodeTape + Signal Finder logistics layer
· Each filled bag gets a Paper Tag: location · count · material type · timestamp · initials
· CodeTape log: where bags went, who moved them, how many remain
· Signal Finder integration: bag counts visible to steward and responders
· Local steward holds the record — no cloud, no app dependency

svg.text: T3
Tier 4 — AI-assisted planning (optional future layer)
· Material volume estimation: bags needed per levee length or erosion site
· Crew routing and logistics suggestions from CodeTape + Signal Finder data
· Emergency coordination: prioritise by area, need, and resource
· Only meaningful when Tier 0–3 data exists and is trustworthy
T4
[ do not build Tier 4 before Tier 0 can fill one sack cleanly ]
What counts as a measurable improvement (any one is enough)
Time Tier 0 fills 20 sacks faster than the hand-fill baseline recorded on the Field Test Sheet
Strain Crew reports less back, shoulder, wrist, or knee strain than baseline
Spills Fewer failed fills or rework sacks than baseline
Access Makes the work possible for a person who could not do the hand-fill baseline
Use the Spoovel v0 Field Test Sheet to record the baseline and the result before deciding.
If it does not improve the baseline in any measurable way — fix the tool, not the test.
